name: alttpr
game: The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past

options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: world_mode
options.description:
Open: Start from Link's House or Sanctuary, without needing to save Zelda in Hyrule Castle
Standard: Start at Link's House and save Zelda in Hyrule Castle before accessing the rest of the world
Inverted: The Light World and Dark World have been flipped.
    - Link starts at their house (swapped with the Bomb Shop) or Dark Sanctuary
    - All Dark World portals now take you to the Light World
    - Link is a bunny in the Light World unless you have the Moon Pearl
    - Agahnim's Tower and Ganon's Tower have swapped places. Agahnim's Tower now requires crystals to enter
    - Ganon is hiding in a new hole on top of Hyrule Castle
    - The Magic Mirror takes you from the Light World to the Dark World
    - The Flute must be activated in Kakariko Village, but will then take you to locations across the Dark World
    - Light World terrain has been modified so that mirror-locked locations can be reached without the mirror
    - The top of Turtle Rock can be accessed by jumping from its tail

options.name: goal
options.description:
Sets the goal for this seed.

crystals: Collect the required number of crystals and kill Ganon.
ganon: Collect the required number of crystals, kill Agahnim on top of Ganon's Tower, then kill Ganon.
dungeons: Complete all 12 dungeons, including Agahnim's Tower and Ganon's Tower, then kill Ganon.
pedestal: Collect all 3 pendants and pull the Master Sword from its pedestal.
triforcehunt: Collect the required number of pieces of the Triforce, then talk to Murahdahla outside Hyrule Castle.
ganonhunt: Collect the required number of pieces of the Triforce, then kill Ganon.
trinity: Either kill Ganon, pull the pedestal, or collect the required number of Triforce pieces and talk to Murahdahla outside Hyrule Castle.
completionist: Collect every check in the game, then kill Ganon.

options.name: open_pyramid
options.description: Whether the Pyramid hole leading to Ganon should be open at the start. Choosing "auto" will open or close it based on your goal setting.

options.name: crystals_needed_for_ganons_tower
options.description: How many crystals are needed to enter Ganon's Tower

options.name: crystals_needed_for_ganon
options.description: How many crystals are needed before Ganon can be killed

options.name: triforce_hunt_goal
options.description: How many Triforce Pieces are required to beat the game when the goal is set to Triforce Hunt or Ganon Hunt

options.name: triforce_hunt_total
options.description: How many Triforce Pieces are in the item pool when the goal is set to Triforce Hunt or Ganon Hunt

options.name: map_shuffle
options.description: Maps can now appear outside of their dungeon. The map screen will not show if a dungeon gives a pendant or crystal until its map has been found.

options.name: compass_shuffle
options.description: Compasses can now appear outside of their dungeon.

options.name: small_key_shuffle
options.description: Small keys can now appear outside of their dungeon.

options.name: big_key_shuffle
options.description: Big keys can now appear outside of their dungeon.

options.name: key_drop_shuffle
options.description: Shuffle keys that are dropped by enemies or hidden under pots.

options.name: entrance_shuffle
options.description: Randomize where each building, cave, and dungeon entrance leads to.

options.name: zelgawoods
options.description:
If entrance shuffle is enabled, add Skull Woods entrances and dropdowns to the entrance shuffle. The main Skull Woods entrance/big chest
dropdown and the second Skull Woods entrance/the dropdown in the back are both added to the dropdown pool. The other two dropdowns in the
front are vanilla, and at least one of the entrances in the back of the Skull Woods area must be a connector.

options.name: enemy_shuffle
options.description:
All enemies except bosses are randomized. Logical enemy shuffle might require defeating enemies that
require specific items (Eyegore, Freezors, etc.) to progress in a dungeon.

options.name: boss_shuffle
options.description:
Bosses are randomized. This includes the Armos/Lanmolas/Moldorm rematches in Ganon's Tower, but not Ganon or either Aganhim fight. Some bosses cannot appear in some locations.

* Vanilla: Bosses are in their original locations.
* Simple: Bosses are shuffled randomly. Armos Knights, Lanmolas, and Moldorm will be fought twice.
* Full: Bosses are shuffled randomly, and three random bosses will be fought twice.
* Chaos: Bosses are shuffled randomly, and any boss can be fought any number of times.

options.name: shopsanity
options.description:
All shops contain randomized items, including Potion Shop and Capacity Upgrade Fairy. Adds 32 items to the item pool. Each type of potion can be purchased
at a random shop.

options.name: prize_shuffle
options.description: Adds Pendants and Crystals to the itempool.

options.name: flute_shuffle
options.description: Randomize the Flute spot destinations. Balanced will spread the Flute spots around the overworld, while Chaos will place them randomly.

options.name: pre_activated_flute
options.description: The Flute does not need to be activated at the village statue after finding it.

options.name: pseudoboots
options.description: Psuedoboots give Link the ability to dash like Pegasus Boots, but they cannot bonk rocks, open King's Tomb, knock items off torches/the Library, or clear small gaps

options.name: mirror_scroll
options.description: Mirror Scroll is an inventory item that warps Link to the start of their current dungeon, and is replaced upon finding the Magic Mirror.

options.name: sprite
options.description:
A custom sprite to use for Link. Must be 'Link' or the exact name of a sprite listed at https://alttpr.com/en/sprite_preview.
If an error occurs when loading the sprite, the default Link sprite will be used.

options.name: heart_beep_rate
options.description: The rate at which heart beeps are played when Link is at low health.

options.name: heart_color
options.description: The color of Link's heart meter.

options.name: fast_menu
options.description: The rate at which the menu opens and closes.

options.name: disable_music
options.description: Disables game music.

options.name: msu_resume
options.description: While using an MSU, when entering and leaving a building/cave/dungeon, the overworld music will pick up where it left off, instead of restarting every time.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
